title: Greedy Algorithms for Joint Sparse Recovery
problem_statement: Extend greedy sparse recovery algorithms from the single measurement vector setting to the multiple measurement vector setting and analyze their recovery guarantees and empirical performance.

guarantees: exact recovery guarantees in jointly sparse setting

main_findings: Normalized variants improve performance over fixed-step variants, especially in MMV.
Performance improves as the number of jointly sparse measurement vectors increases.
Under Gaussian matrices, SCoSaMP often achieves stronger empirical weak phase transitions.
SNIHT, SNHTP, and SCoSaMP empirically outperform RA-SOMP+MUSIC in weak phase transition comparisons.
Worst-case ARIP/RIP sufficient conditions are pessimistic relative to empirical behavior.
limitations_or_caveats: Average-case theory for these greedy thresholding algorithms is not fully available.
Theoretical sufficient conditions are more conservative than observed empirical performance.
Some benefits depend on the measurement ensemble.

recommended_ai_ingestion_strategy.why: This combination supports human verification, direct LLM reading, vector retrieval, and metadata routing.
